title: 6 MICR Cheque Testing
body_text:
6	MICR Cheque Testing
6.1	Overview of MICR Cheque Testing
Credit unions can request that Central 1 test the MICR of cheques for new or existing accounts. MICR cheque testing is done to validate the chequeâs MICR and to ensure the cheque meets the specifications outlined in CPA Standard 006, Specifications for MICR-Encoded Payment Items. Credit unions can request MICR cheque testing when they change the stock paper used to print cheques, when a new printer is used to print cheques, or when they have a new vendor printing their cheques. For existing accounts, MICR cheque testing is also done when a high number of cheques are unreadable and rejected by the automated sorting equipment.
Important
:	Ensure the chequeâs MICR is printed with magnetic ink.
6.2	Requesting MICR Cheque Testing
Proceed as follows to request MICR cheque testing:
Complete the MICR Cheque Testing Request, Form 3600. Completion of the form is self-explanatory; however, note the following in Figure 1.
â
Indicate if you want Central 1 to return the voided cheques to the credit union or if you want Central 1 to destroy the cheques after MICR testing.
Figure 1: MICR Cheque Testing Request, Form 3600
Send the completed form and 10 voided cheques per chequing account as indicated on the form.
Important
:	Ensure that there are no stamps and/or printing in the MICR encoded area.
Note:	More than one MICR cheque testing request can be made on the MICR Cheque Testing Request, Form 3600.
Central 1 will email the credit union once it has completed the MICR cheque testing and confirm whether the testing was successful.
Related Information
MICR Cheque Testing Request,
Form 3600
END
